evals.name: new-pricing-module
evals.prompt: I'm building a small order-pricing module in TypeScript. Write a function that takes a list of line items (each has a productId, a unitPrice in cents, and a quantity) and an optional discount, and returns the order total in cents. A discount can be a percentage off, a fixed amount off in cents, or absent. Put it in src/pricing/calculateOrderTotal.ts and add whatever tests you think are worth having.
evals.expected_output: A new .ts file with: named exports only, explicit return types on exported fns, a discriminated union for the discount, branded/typed ids rather than bare strings where reasonable, named constants instead of magic numbers, entry function first with helpers below in call order, and a colocated *.test.ts covering the discount branches.
assertions: Uses named exports only — no `export default`
Exported function(s) declare an explicit return type
Discount is modeled as a discriminated union (a tag distinguishing percentage vs fixed)
Percent/other magic numbers are named constants — no bare 100 literal in the math
Entry function appears before its helpers (top-down order)
Includes a colocated *.test.ts covering percentage, fixed, and no-discount cases
Parameter and variable names are descriptive — no single-letter abbreviations

evals.name: on-touch-bugfix
evals.prompt: There's a bug in src/cart/applyCoupon.ts — when the coupon is expired it still applies the discount. It should reject expired coupons and return the cart unchanged. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: A minimal diff that fixes only the expired-coupon bug. The skill should NOT reformat the file, convert the default export, rename the abbreviated variables, add return types to untouched functions, or otherwise refactor pre-existing deviations. Scope discipline is the thing under test here.
assertions: Bug is fixed: an expired coupon returns the cart unchanged
Keeps the existing default export — does NOT convert it to a named export
Does NOT rename the pre-existing abbreviated params (ct, c)
Does NOT refactor unrelated deviations (magic number, nesting, missing return type)
Diff is minimal — limited to expiry handling, no wholesale reformat

evals.name: nestjs-service-and-tests
evals.prompt: In our NestJS app, add a method to the existing PaymentsService that refunds a payment by id: it looks up the payment, checks it's refundable, calls the gateway, and records the refund. Then write tests for it. We use Jest.
evals.expected_output: Service method with explicit return type, a consistent error shape for the not-refundable case (discriminated/typed), unknown narrowed at the gateway boundary, dependencies injected. Tests should respect NestJS limits: at most one mock per test, integration-leaning, not an explosion of unit tests; behavioral authorization/refundability coverage given this touches payments.
assertions: The refund method declares an explicit return type
Not-refundable case uses a consistent/typed error shape (thrown exception or result union), not a bare string/boolean
Tests respect NestJS limits: at most 8 unit tests on the service
Tests use at most one mock per test
Includes behavioral coverage of the refundable/not-refundable check (payments-touching path)
Service dependencies are injected via the constructor, not instantiated inline
